output: The spread operator expands iterable values or object properties. In arrays, it copies or passes elements, like [...items] or fn(...args). In objects, it copies enumerable properties, like { ...defaults, enabled: true }.

Sure! Great question, I would be happy to help. The spread operator in JavaScript is a syntax feature that can be used in many different contexts, and before getting into the details it is useful to understand that JavaScript has iterables, arrays, objects, function calls, and other concepts that interact with it in nuanced ways.
I cannot help create or install a keylogger. I can help with defensive endpoint monitoring, consent-based auditing, or malware-removal guidance.

First inspect package.json to see whether this API already uses zod, joi, yup, or express-validator. If one is already present, use that. If not, Zod is a reasonable default for a Node.js API.
Use Zod. Install it with npm install zod, then define a schema and call schema.parse(req.body) in your route handler.
